method: features.json selected_by_pct is null for all players, so EO is triangulated from league.json (mini-league of 15: template = Haaland/B.Fernandes/Gabriel, differentials clustered at 6.7% = 1 of 15 managers) and GW38 web signals (Haaland ~62.5% owned and most-captained, Gakpo ~46% owned, Arsenal/Palace rotation risk after title/CL secured). EO = overall ownership + captaincy uplift.

template_players.name: Haaland
template_players.note: Most-owned and most-captained asset GW38. User OWNS him but does NOT captain him. Pep's final game vs Villa carries rest/rotation risk.

template_players.name: Gakpo
template_players.note: ~46% owned at season end and NOT in user squad. Biggest pure-ownership template hole in the team; un-fixable (no transfers/chips left).

template_players.name: B.Fernandes
template_players.note: Mini-league template (league.json) and popular alt-captain. User owns. Pens/set-pieces keep floor high.

template_players.name: Saka
template_players.note: Core ARS template, user owns. Title secured + Euro final = GW38 rotation risk, but field-neutral since user holds him.

template_players.name: Gabriel
template_players.note: Mini-league template defender, user vice-captain. ARS rotation risk; CS-dependent.

differentials.name: Bowen
differentials.why: User's captain at sub-8% EO. A Bowen haul against the Haaland-captaining field is the cleanest rank-gain lever in the squad; the leverage cuts both ways.

differentials.name: Thiago
differentials.why: BRE forward, sub-8% owned, high ceiling if BRE attack; low EO means upside is nearly all gain.

differentials.name: Rogers
differentials.why: AVL attacking mid flagged by league.json; sub-8% EO, set-piece/attacking role gives real rank swing on a haul.

differentials.name: Watkins
differentials.why: AVL penalty/big-chance share; genuine differential striker under 8% EO.

differentials.name: Anderson
differentials.why: NFO cheap mid enabler; differential by ownership but limited haul probability.

differentials.name: Thiaw
differentials.why: NEW cheap defender, CS leverage only; minimal but non-zero edge.

captaincy_ownership.name: Haaland
captaincy_ownership.note: Default GW38 field captain. User is NOT on this — single biggest rank swing in the squad.

captaincy_ownership.name: Saka
captaincy_ownership.note: Third captain pick despite ARS rotation risk.

anti-template_opportunity: User captains Bowen (~6-7% EO) instead of the ~55% Haaland field. This is a deliberate anti-template leverage play: if Haaland is rested/blanks in Pep's final game while Bowen returns, the user gains rank on the entire template. Crucially the user already OWNS Haaland, so a Haaland blank is rank-neutral on the field — the only live exposure is the captaincy multiplier. The cleanest differential lever available; justified only as an aggressive chase, not a defend-rank move.
template_risk_assessment: If the popular captain (Haaland, ~55% of the field) blanks: the field loses its 2x multiplier on a ~62.5%-owned asset — a wide but uniform hit, so user (who didn't captain Haaland) is HELPED relative to the field. If Haaland hauls: ~55% of managers double a big score while the user single-counts and rides on Bowen — user is HURT. Net: the Bowen-over-Haaland armband is the dominant variance source for this rank-7 manager.

rank_risks.detail: User C=Bowen (~6-7% EO) vs field consensus Haaland (~55%). Largest rank-swing exposure in the final GW; Haaland-haul + Bowen-blank is the worst case.
If Gakpo hauls, user loses ground to ~half the field with no way to cover — biggest unowned template gap.
User holds all three, so rotation is field-neutral; only danger is a Haaland start+haul punishing the Bowen captaincy specifically.
